quick_facts.label: What it is
quick_facts.value: M.Des is a postgraduate design degree, but its structure and specialisations are institution-specific. For example, NID offers full-time M.Des programmes across communication, industrial, IT-integrated, interdisciplinary, and textile/apparel/lifestyle design streams; IIT Bombay lists industrial, communication, animation, interaction, and mobility & vehicle design streams.

quick_facts.label: Typical duration
quick_facts.value: The common two-year description is a useful baseline for some programmes, but it is not universal: IIT Bombay describes a first year followed by a second year focused largely on design projects, while NID’s full-time M.Des is two-and-a-half years.

quick_facts.label: Entry qualification
quick_facts.value: Requirements differ by institution. NID’s 2026–27 M.Des cycle accepted a bachelor’s degree or equivalent in any discipline, subject to its published age and document rules. CEED 2026 permitted applicants with a minimum three-year post-10+2 degree/diploma/postgraduate programme (or specified final-year/GD Arts routes) to take the examination.

quick_facts.label: Main admission routes
quick_facts.value: Two major routes are institute-specific NID DAT (DAT Prelims followed by DAT Mains) and CEED for participating IIT/IISc/IIITDM and other result-sharing institutes. A qualifying CEED score alone does not secure admission; institutes may apply further tests, interviews, and their own eligibility checks.

quick_facts.label: Examples of specialisation
quick_facts.value: Current official listings include product design, interaction design, visual design, industrial design, integrated product design, XR design, communication design, animation, mobility/vehicle design, transportation and automobile design, strategic design management, and textile/apparel/lifestyle disciplines.

quick_facts.label: Further-study pathway
quick_facts.value: Design doctoral study is a direct academic progression at several CEED-linked institutions: the CEED 2026 brochure lists Ph.D. in Design opportunities at multiple participating institutions, alongside M.Des programmes.

sections.heading: Eligibility: check the institute, not only the degree title
paragraphs: There is no single India-wide M.Des admission rule. In NID’s 2026–27 cycle, an applicant needed a bachelor’s degree or equivalent in any discipline from a recognised institution (with specified age limits and provisions for final-year candidates). This demonstrates that an undergraduate design degree is not universally required.

For CEED 2026, the examination-level eligibility was broader than a specific design bachelor’s degree: candidates could have completed, or be completing by July 2026, a minimum three-year post-10+2 degree, diploma, or postgraduate programme; the brochure also recognised the specified GD Arts diploma route. This made candidates eligible to sit CEED, but individual admitting institutions retained authority to impose programme-specific conditions.

Applicants should therefore verify three separate layers: the entrance-exam rule, the institute’s academic eligibility rule, and any stream/portfolio/task requirement. At IIT Bombay, for example, the 2026 M.Des page states that CEED-qualified candidates meet its base eligibility, while final selection involved written test and/or interview processes.
bullets: A B.Des, B.Arch, interior-design, visual-communication, engineering, fine-arts, or other bachelor’s background may be relevant depending on the institution; do not assume all schools treat them identically.
For international qualifications, NID’s 2026–27 handbook required an equivalence certificate from AIU or the Ministry of Education at document-upload stage.
Always use the admission notice for the relevant intake: age limits, accepted qualifications, portfolio expectations, and category documentation can change between cycles.

sections.heading: Duration: commonly two years, but not a fixed national standard
paragraphs: M.Des duration varies by awarding institute. IIT Bombay’s course description frames the programme across a first year and a second year, with the latter spent largely on design projects. In contrast, NID explicitly describes its full-time M.Des as a two-and-a-half-year programme. Students should use the chosen institute’s academic handbook rather than rely on the generic “two-year” label.

Mode can vary too. IIT Bombay’s 2026 admissions page stated that its M.Des and M.Des by Research admissions were possible in full-time or part-time modes; this should not be generalised to every M.Des provider.
bullets: Confirm duration, academic calendar, and mode of study in the programme-specific regulations.
Do not confuse a taught M.Des with M.Des by Research: IIT Bombay separately describes its M.Des by Research duration as flexible, from 1.5 to 3 years.

sections.heading: Curriculum: studio, theory, electives, projects, and a design-research component
paragraphs: An M.Des is normally a specialised, project-led programme rather than a uniform syllabus. IIT Bombay’s official M.Des description says that the first two semesters combine theory courses with learning/instruction-oriented projects; students can take electives in the second and third semesters. The second year includes three application-oriented projects and a special project oriented towards design research.

Specialisation determines the disciplinary lens and the types of projects undertaken. NID’s 2026–27 offerings illustrate the breadth: graphic design and animation; product, furniture/interior, toy/game, transportation/automobile, and universal design; interaction, information, and new-media design; retail-experience and strategic-design-management; and apparel, accessory, and textile design.

Prospective students should inspect studio/course lists, faculty expertise, lab access, project briefs, and graduation requirements for the exact discipline—not merely the institution-wide M.Des label.
bullets: Common learning components in the IIT Bombay example: perceptual abilities, skills and techniques, social/general awareness, electives, and applied design projects.

Examples of IIT Bombay elective/specialisation areas listed by the school include product detailing and visualisation, ergonomics, exhibition design, typography, video, photography, animation, multimedia, information design, computer graphics, and illustration.

sections.heading: Admission: CEED and NID DAT are distinct pathways
paragraphs: CEED is conducted by IIT Bombay as a qualifying examination for M.Des programmes at listed IITs, IISc Bangalore, IIITDM institutions, and additional result-sharing institutes. It assesses design aptitude including visual perception, drawing, logical reasoning, creativity, communication, and problem solving. CEED qualification and score do not themselves guarantee a seat: candidates must apply to each institute and may face further tests and/or interviews.

NID uses its own two-stage Design Aptitude Test process: DAT Prelims followed by DAT Mains for shortlisted candidates. For the 2026–27 M.Des cycle, NID calculated final marks from DAT Mains Studio Test (40%) and Interview (60%).

As of the requested review date, July 19, 2026, the CEED 2026 examination and its published application period are already past: the official brochure scheduled the exam for January 18, 2026, results for March 4, 2026, and score-card download through July 31, 2026. Future applicants should wait for the official CEED 2027 and institution-specific notices rather than reuse 2026 deadlines.
bullets: For an IIT Bombay M.Des application in 2026, CEED-qualified applicants also completed stream/program-specific tasks, followed by selection stages including written test and interview.
At NID, applicants could select up to two disciplines only within one faculty stream in the 2026–27 cycle; discipline preferences and seat allotment rules were cycle-specific.

sections.heading: Costs: compare the published fee circulars by institution, category, and year
paragraphs: Fees are highly institution-, category-, residency-, and semester-dependent. For a concrete 2026–27 example, NID listed a non-refundable M.Des application fee of ₹3,000 for one discipline for General/Gen-EWS/OBC-NCL Indian applicants, ₹2,000 for eligible female applicants, ₹1,500 for SC/ST applicants, and ₹500 for PwD or third-gender applicants; applying to two eligible disciplines doubled the corresponding listed fee. These are application fees, not the total programme cost.

For IIT Bombay’s Autumn Semester 2026–27 new-entrant master’s cohort, its fee circular included M.Des among the covered programmes and listed different first-semester totals by fee group/category, including ₹52,150 for Group I, ₹94,150 for Group II, and ₹34,150 for SC/ST/PwD. The same circular says the institute may revise fees in subsequent semesters. Accommodation, mess, tools, materials, travel, software, and project-prototyping costs should be budgeted separately where applicable.
bullets: Before applying, distinguish application fee, acceptance/token payment, one-time charges, refundable deposits, tuition/semester fees, hostel charges, mess advance, and discipline-specific material costs.
Use the current year’s official fee circular; do not treat a previous intake’s fee as a quote for a future cohort.

sections.heading: After M.Des: design research, doctoral study, and specialised practice
paragraphs: For an academic/research route, Ph.D. in Design is offered at multiple CEED-linked institutions. The CEED 2026 programme list, for example, identifies Ph.D. in Design options at IISc Bangalore, IIT Bombay, IIT Delhi, IIT Guwahati, IIT Hyderabad, IIT Kanpur, IIT Roorkee, and IIITDM Kancheepuram, among others listed in that cycle.

A research-oriented master’s alternative also exists at some institutions. IIT Bombay offers M.Des by Research separately from its taught M.Des, with a published flexible duration of 1.5 to 3 years; applicants should assess whether their goals are better served by advanced studio practice, research training, or a doctorate.

For professional pathways, the specialisation and project portfolio are central: official programme offerings span interaction, product, communication/graphic, animation, mobility/vehicle, service-adjacent strategic design, information, and other design domains. The exact job title is determined by the employer and the individual’s skills and portfolio, not automatically by the M.Des degree name.
bullets: Potential academic progression: Ph.D. in Design, subject to each university’s admission rules.

Potential practice direction: build a portfolio around the selected discipline’s studio and applied projects; specialised programmes are available across product, communication, interaction, mobility, information, and related design fields.

UGC-NET is a separate national examination route administered by NTA for outcomes including Junior Research Fellowship/Assistant Professor eligibility and Ph.D.-admission categories; eligibility and subject requirements must be checked in the relevant current NTA bulletin.
